bytes.topic: Service Accounts & Roles — machine identities, workload identity federation, no long-lived keys
bytes.title: iam Service Accounts & Roles — Machine Identities with Workload Identity Federation
bytes.body: iam service accounts are used to identify machine identities, while roles define the permissions for these identities. To manage machine identities securely, iam provides workload identity federation, which eliminates the need for long-lived keys. With workload identity federation, you can configure a workload identity pool to manage service accounts and roles. This approach ensures that service accounts are authenticated and authorized without using long-lived credentials. The iam API provides methods to create and manage service accounts, roles, and workload identity pools, making it easier to implement machine identity management.
bytes.code_snippet:
gcloud iam service-accounts create my-service-account --description 'My Service Account'
gcloud iam roles create my-role --description 'My Role'
gcloud iam workload-identity-pools create my-pool --description 'My Pool'
gcloud iam service-accounts add-iam-policy-binding my-service-account --member 'serviceAccount:my-service-account@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com' --role 'projects/my-project/roles/my-role'

bytes.topic: Zero Trust Architecture — never trust, always verify, microsegmentation
bytes.title: iam Zero Trust Architecture — Always Verify Microsegments
bytes.body: To enforce zero trust in iam, create a microsegmented architecture by defining granular IAM policies. Use iam's Conditions feature to specify IP ranges, user attributes, and resource properties. For example, allow access to a specific S3 bucket only when the request originates from a trusted IP range: `{ "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "s3:GetObject", "Resource": "arn:aws:s3:::example-bucket/*", "Condition": { "IpAddress": { "aws:SourceIp": [ "192.0.2.1/32" ] } } } ] }`

bytes.topic: Cloud Security Posture — CSPM tools, detecting misconfigurations at scale
bytes.title: IAM: Detecting Misconfigurations at Scale with IAM Cloud Security Posture
bytes.body: IAM provides a built-in feature called Cloud Security Posture, which utilizes AWS Config to track and monitor resource configurations. You can leverage AWS Config Rules to detect and remediate misconfigurations at scale. To create a Config Rule in IAM, use the `CreateConfigRule` API. The rule can be triggered by a specific resource or a service-linked role. You can also define a threshold to trigger remediation actions.

bytes.topic: Container Security — pod security standards, runtime security with Falco
bytes.title: iam: Enforcing Pod Security Standards with Falco
bytes.body:
To ensure container security in iam, you can leverage Falco's runtime security capabilities. Start by installing the Falco operator in your iam cluster: `oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/falcosecurity/falco-operator/main/deploy/operator.yaml`. Then, configure the Falco operator to monitor your pods using the `falco-config` CR: `oc apply -f - <<EOF`.
`apiVersion: falco.org/v1alpha1
kind: FalcoConfig
metadata:
  name: falco-config
spec:
  rules:  - selector: {}
    output: allow
    severity: LOW
    desc: 'allow all pods'
EOF`.
This allows Falco to monitor your pods and enforce pod security standards.
bytes.code_snippet:
oc apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: falco.org/v1alpha1
kind: FalcoConfig
metadata:
  name: falco-config
spec:
  rules:  - selector: {}
    output: allow
    severity: LOW
    desc: 'allow all pods'
EOF
